Text: What is the primary purpose of a load balancer?
Options: Encrypt traffic between services;Distribute incoming requests across multiple servers to prevent overload;Cache API responses;Monitor server CPU usage

Text: What does CDN stand for and what does it do?
Options: Central Data Node — stores logs centrally;Content Delivery Network — serves static assets from geographically distributed edge servers;Content Distribution Node — replicates databases;Core DNS Network — routes domain lookups

Text: What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases?
Options: SQL is faster than NoSQL in all scenarios;SQL uses structured schemas with ACID transactions while NoSQL is schema-flexible and trades some consistency for scale;NoSQL supports joins and transactions better than SQL;SQL and NoSQL are identical in design

Data is always consistent across all nodes immediately;All replicas will converge to the same value given enough time with no new updates;Data is consistent only during read operations;A model where writes are rejected until all nodes agree

Text: What is the purpose of a message queue like Kafka or RabbitMQ?
Options: To store relational data;To decouple producers and consumers enabling async communication and buffering bursts of traffic;To cache database queries;To handle user authentication tokens
Encrypting database partitions;Horizontally partitioning data across multiple databases each holding a subset of rows;Vertically splitting tables by column;Replicating a database to a standby server

Text: What is the purpose of a circuit breaker pattern?
Options: To limit API rate;To prevent cascading failures by stopping requests to a failing service after a threshold is exceeded;To encrypt service communication;To route traffic between microservices

Text: What is the difference between synchronous and asynchronous communication between services?
Options: Sync is always faster;Sync waits for a response blocking the caller while async sends a message and continues without waiting;Async requires HTTP;Sync uses message queues

Text: What is a write-through cache strategy?
Options: Writing only to the cache and evicting later;Writing to both the cache and the underlying database simultaneously on every write;Writing to the database first then invalidating cache;A cache that only handles write operations

Text: What is the role of a reverse proxy?
Options: It forwards client requests to origin servers and returns responses hiding the server topology;It proxies outbound requests from internal services;It balances traffic between DNS servers;It caches only static files
